name: the primitive type called up

VINIT.justification: the state before any ratio or mapping cardinality is assigned

TANCH.justification: the final type-checked state of the relation

AFWD.justification: the transformation from a single source to multiple targets

AREV.justification: the transformation from multiple sources back to a single target

CLINK.justification: the chaining of multiple n:m mappings into a single stoichiometric path

IMSCRIB.justification: the recognition of the relation as a distinct stoichiometric type

FSPLIT.justification: the branching of a relation into source-heavy and target-heavy paths

FFUSE.justification: the unification of divergent paths back into a single n:m relation

EVALT.justification: the confirmation that n does not equal m

EVALF.justification: the detection of a 1:1 or n:n symmetry which violates the 'up' type

ENGAGR.justification: a state where the ratio is fluctuating or undefined between matched and unmatched

IFIX.justification: the permanent assignment of the 'up' label to the relation

steps.domain_action: identify the relation as a candidate for stoichiometric typing
branch the relation to evaluate its source and target counts
confirm the source count n is distinct from target count m
map the forward transformation of the n:m set
map the reverse transformation of the m:n set
reconstitute the n:m relation from its directional components
branch to check for strict inequality
reject the path if n equals m
return to the primary stoichiometric evaluation
compose the validated n:m mapping into the type signature
fix the 'up' stoichiometric type in the permanent record

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the Lean type checker and stoichiometry inductive definition
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: internal port routing between source and target sets
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the stoichiometric register storing n and m values
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the ordinal ordering of F3 primitives

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the operation merely labels an existing structural property without adding information

notes: Auto-designed from: the primitive type called up
